mnemonics: BALANCE your Scale: Use Sampling, Cost, Algorithms, New metrics, Evaluation to handle Unbalanced Datasets
keyPoints: Unbalanced datasets occur when one class has significantly more samples than others, leading to biased models.

Techniques to address imbalance include resampling (oversampling minority, undersampling majority), using different performance metrics (Precision, Recall, F1-Score, ROC-AUC), and algorithmic approaches (cost-sensitive learning).
Simply aiming for high accuracy is a major pitfall; a model can achieve high accuracy by always predicting the majority class, which is useless for identifying the critical minority class.
commonMistakes: Relying solely on Accuracy as a performance metric, which is misleading for unbalanced data.
Applying resampling techniques (like SMOTE) without proper cross-validation, potentially leading to overfitting and data leakage.
Ignoring the problem entirely and expecting the model to learn the rare patterns without any special treatment.
